title: 'Backtracked from his word': Minister says Rahul changed demand after govt agreed to NEET debate
content: Congress leaders marched to Prime Minister's residence demanding his and the Education Minister's resignation.  They protested brutalities against students and demanded accountability for examination irregularities.  Police detained several senior Congress leaders during the demonstration in New Delhi.  Rahul and Priyanka Gandhi met injured students admitted at Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital.  The party stated students' demands are clear regarding paper leaks and ministerial responsibility.

title: 'Ghor paap': PM Modi on NEET paper leak; vows strict action against culprits
content: More than 170 people, including CJP protesters and Delhi Police personnel, were treated at central government hospitals on Monday following clashes during the 'Chalo Sansad' march, with a young woman admitted to Safdarjung Hospital in critical condition, sources said. According to hospital sources, around 150 injured people were taken to Dr Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital, while at least 24 others received treatment at Lady Hardinge Medical College (LHMC).

title: Justin Langer frontrunner for England coach job; Shastri, Dravid not in the race
content: Former Australia coach Justin Langer has emerged as the frontrunner to replace Brendon McCullum as England's Test coach. While the ECB hopes to make an appointment before next month's Pakistan series, former India coaches Ravi Shastri and Rahul Dravid are not part of the recruitment process.

title: Another oil shock loading for India? How Red Sea disruptions may hit Russian crude supply
content: Iran-backed Yemeni group, the Houthis, have threatened a maritime blockade on Saudi Arabia. What this essentially means is that Saudi Arabia’s crude oil exports are set to be hit. And India which gets a big portion of its crude imports from the Middle East country.

title: Intel to cut jobs in Data Center and AI Group: What the chipmaker said
content: Intel is reportedly embarking on another round of layoffs within its data center group as part of a strategy to enhance efficiency and agility through better organizational alignment. Despite these job cuts, product commitments and roadmaps are expected to remain intact. Over the last four years, Intel's workforce has seen a significant decline, and the chipmaker is set to unveil its quarterly financial results this week.

title: 'Criticising him isn't justified': Sikandar Raza defends Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza believes Vaibhav Sooryavanshi can become a generational talent. He urged patience for the 15-year-old after his challenging debut series. Raza highlighted the importance of proper management for the young cricketer's development. Zimbabwe anticipates an entertaining T20I series against India. Both teams are in transition, making the contest more evenly balanced.

title: Jeff Bezos on returning to work after retirement: I just couldn’t sit on sidelines
content: Jeff Bezos has returned to an operational role to launch his new AI startup, Prometheus.  He describes this demanding work as rewarding, which he calls "Type 2 fun."  Bezos believes artificial intelligence will create a labor shortage, not job losses.  His new venture has secured significant funding and is now his primary focus.  He is energized by the long road ahead as Prometheus scales its operations.

title: How constant niggles and breakdowns are hampering 2027 ODI World Cup plans
content: India's injury list is growing: Nitish Kumar Reddy injured himself during the ODI series against Afghanistan last month, Hardik Pandya has yet to feature in an ODI for India since the Champions Trophy final against New Zealand in March 2025. Harshit Rana has suffered two injury setbacks in the last six months. Washington Sundar has had a couple of breakdowns in the same period. Varun Chakravarthy suffered a hairline fracture before a Grade 2 hamstring injury ruled him out of action.

title: Texas cave’s rare aquifer tour returns after flood raises water 30 feet
content: Severe Texas flooding has raised water levels inside Natural Bridge Caverns significantly. This unusual event has temporarily revived a special guided aquifer tour for visitors. The tour allows people to see parts of the cave normally inaccessible due to dry conditions. It highlights the vital role of groundwater in supplying water to central Texas communities. This rare opportunity offers a unique glimpse into the state's underground natural systems.

title: From Black Mirror to real life: AI lets people talk to dead loved ones
content: With the advent of AI chatbots, people can now interact with digital renditions of those they've lost. These 'griefbots' serve to bring emotional healing and a sense of resolution to many, fulfilling critical emotional and social needs during the grieving process. Yet, their imperfections raise questions about overreliance on these interactions, as well as the broader ethical ramifications of employing such technology in human relationships.

title: China to country’s AI startups and chipmakers: Do not let tech fall into Western hands
content: Chinese regulators are reportedly considering stricter export controls on AI technology.  This move follows the cancellation of a significant acquisition deal by Beijing.  The government aims to prevent Western firms from acquiring advanced Chinese AI capabilities.  They also seek to restrict overseas manufacturing of proprietary advanced semiconductor designs.  However, tech leaders warn that over-regulation could hinder China's AI development.

title: A look at Leonardo DiCaprio's multi-million-dollar property portfolio
content: Leonardo DiCaprio boasts a remarkable real estate portfolio that spans the United States and Belize. His assets feature a lavish compound in Hollywood Hills, a tranquil private island in Belize, chic apartments in New York City, and a sprawling estate in Palm Springs. Furthermore, DiCaprio has acquired a stunning Spanish Colonial home in Los Feliz, along with a picturesque beachfront property in Malibu and a stylish residence in Beverly Hills.

title: The future of flying? US speeds up plans for air taxis, supersonic planes and drones
content: The FAA is updating aviation rules to encourage new technologies.  It supports air taxis and supersonic passenger flights with new regulations.  Pilot programs are preparing for the safe integration of electric air taxis.  Companies are developing charging infrastructure for electric aircraft operations.  The agency also aims to expand drone use and commercial space launches.

title: 'When I needed someone, he was there': PV Sindhu recalls Kohli's role in comeback
content: PV Sindhu received crucial guidance from Virat Kohli during a difficult career period.  This conversation helped her overcome a serious injury and focus on rehabilitation.  Her husband facilitated her recovery away from public scrutiny in Atlanta.  Sindhu then achieved a historic Japan Open women's singles title win.  This victory marked a remarkable comeback after her extensive recovery process.

title: Qatar Airways denied boarding to infant; court awards family Rs 10 lakh
content: A Kerala consumer commission ordered Qatar Airways to pay ten lakh rupees compensation.  The airline denied boarding to two children, including an infant, despite issuing boarding passes.  This action was deemed a deficiency in service and unfair trade practice by the commission.  The family suffered significant mental agony and hardship due to the airline's arbitrary decision.  Qatar Airways must comply with the order within forty-five days.

title: Can your landlord install CCTV camera outside your rented home? Here's what law says
content: Landlords cannot assume unrestricted rights to monitor tenants' private spaces.  Surveillance invading a tenant's private area may violate their privacy rights.  The Supreme Court affirmed privacy as an intrinsic part of personal liberty.  A Calcutta High Court ruling supported tenants' privacy against intrusive cameras.  Laws protect tenants from intentional capture of private areas without consent.

title: Enzo Fernandez reacts after red card in Argentina's World Cup final loss to Spain
content: Enzo Fernandez expressed pride in representing Argentina after their World Cup final defeat.  He highlighted the team's commitment and effort over the final result.  The midfielder thanked fans for their unwavering support throughout the tournament.  He stated that wearing his country's shirt is the greatest honor.  Fernandez pledged to continue giving his all for Argentina.

title: Delhi HC orders immediate transfer of Sonam Wangchuk to Medanta Hospital
content: Sonam Wangchuk received Delhi High Court permission to move hospitals. He is on a hunger strike for the alleged NEET paper leak. The court ordered his transfer to Medanta Hospital for continued treatment. Safdarjung Hospital will provide all necessary medical treatment details. A panel of doctors will oversee his care at the new facility.

title: China conducts rare live-fire drill inside Japan's claimed EEZ
content: China conducted a live-fire naval drill within Japan's claimed exclusive economic zone. Tokyo lodged a diplomatic protest, citing potential navigation dangers and safety concerns. Beijing dismissed Japan's protest, disputing the legal basis for the EEZ claim. This exercise is viewed as part of growing military coordination between China and Russia. The incident revives a long-standing dispute over Okinotorishima's status.

title: One of America's oldest homes is for sale and it comes with a $1.25 million price tag
content: A rare 1678 William Haskell House is for sale in Gloucester, Massachusetts. This historic colonial-era property offers a glimpse into early American architecture. The nearly 350-year-old home retains original features and modern amenities. It was built by William Haskell, who harvested salt hay from local marshes. The estate now includes an antique barn and a covered porch.

title: ‘Won’t give another retake’: Shekhar Suman recalls working with rekha in ‘utsav’:
content: Shekhar Suman fondly recalled his Bollywood debut opposite Rekha in Utsav. He shared how Rekha made him feel comfortable during intimate scenes. Suman also reminisced about working with Madhuri Dixit in his second film. He accepted supporting roles and played a negative character in Naache Mayuri. Audiences disliking his role in Pati Parmeshwar was a compliment.

title: 'Turned his car around and drove away': Mamata's swipe at PM Modi over CJP Parliament march
content: Mamata Banerjee criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi for leaving Parliament during a protest.  A satirical group called the Cockroach Janta Party marched towards Parliament yesterday.  Protesters clashed with police, resulting in injuries on both sides.  The CJP demands the education minister's resignation over various issues.  Banerjee stated she would join the CJP protest if necessary.

title: CJP's 'Sansad Chalo': Young woman injured in police action on ventilator
content: A young woman is on ventilator support after sustaining injuries during a protest. Thousands of CJP supporters marched towards Parliament demanding the education minister's resignation. Police used lathis and tear gas to disperse protesters who tried to breach barricades. CJP accused Delhi Police of excessive force and brutality against peaceful demonstrators. The protest entered its second day with leaders continuing their sit-in and hunger strike.

title: India Test squad for Lanka tour likely this week; selectors sweat over Sundar
content: India's selectors are set to pick the squad for the two-Test tour of Sri Lanka, with Washington Sundar's hamstring injury the biggest selection concern. Jasprit Bumrah and Ravindra Jadeja are expected to return, while decisions on Sundar's replacement and the all-rounder balance remain pending. India need a strong series to boost their World Test Championship qualification hopes.

title: MP high court seeks state’s reply on 'teacherless' government schools
content: Madhya Pradesh High Court has issued notice to the state government regarding schools. Nearly 1,900 government schools are reportedly running without any teachers assigned. An audit revealed 1,895 schools had no teachers, including those with enrolled students. Teachers are also unevenly posted, with some schools having excess staff. The court seeks to address this violation of children's right to education.

title: EXPLAINED: Why Spain players cut the goal nets after winning World Cup
content: Spain celebrated their 2026 FIFA World Cup victory by cutting down goal nets.  This tradition was borrowed from American basketball championship celebrations.  Ferran Torres scored the winning goal in extra time against Argentina.  Spain secured their second World Cup title after a dominant performance.  The players took pieces of the net as souvenirs of their triumph.

title: ‘We are eating leaves’: Assam floods make boy spend night on tree, others on rooftops
content: Torrential runoff from Nagaland triggered devastating floods across Assam's Sivasagar and Charaideo districts. Thousands of families were marooned, with many taking refuge on rooftops and trees. A viral video highlighted the plight of a boy and his family surviving on leaves. Former MLA Debabrata Saikia described the situation as terrifying and critical. Many stranded individuals faced challenges reaching authorities due to communication failures.

title: 'Fight together': Mamata urges INDIA bloc unity against BJP, shuts down door on rebels
content: Trinamool Congress chief Mamata Banerjee declared defectors will not be readmitted to the party. She asserted that many capable individuals exist to replace those who have left. This statement was made during the party's Martyrs' Day rally in Kolkata. The Trinamool Congress faces significant internal organizational challenges and leadership disputes. Several senior leaders and Members of Parliament have recently broken away from the party.

title: SBI Funds Management listing makes some employees crorepatis
content: SBI Funds Management made a strong debut on Dalal Street today, listing at a premium of around 7% over its IPO price. The stock opened at Rs 610 on the BSE, which is a gain of 6.27% from the issue price of Rs 574, while it listed at Rs 613.30 on the NSE.

title: Bike mechanic's faulty repair damaged man's motorcycle; consumer court awards Rs 66,000
content: A Kerala consumer commission ordered a mechanic to pay over sixty-six thousand rupees.  This decision followed a daily wage labourer's motorcycle breaking down shortly after repairs.  The mechanic had quoted ten thousand rupees but took twelve thousand in advance for the work.  The commission found the repair work negligent and a deficiency in service provided.  The labourer suffered financial losses and mental agony due to the faulty repairs.

title: 'Completely un-Indian': Rahul demands PM Modi's apology over students' protest crackdown
content: Rahul Gandhi criticised Prime Minister Modi's silence on police action against students.  Opposition leaders met the Lok Sabha Speaker demanding a parliamentary discussion.  Protesters and police accused each other of violence during demonstrations.  Students are protesting against the education and testing system's failures.  The opposition vows to ensure students' voices are heard in Parliament.

title: Amitabh Bachchan's ICU blog post raises concerns over his health
content: Amitabh Bachchan shared reflections on difficult recovery after hospital stays and surgery. He described the homecoming period as the most challenging phase for patients. The actor previously revealed his liver functions at twenty-five percent after an accident. Despite health setbacks, Bachchan remains actively engaged in films and television. He continues his professional commitments and social media presence.

title: 'British govt wouldn't have lathi-charged like this': Kejriwal on CJP protest
content: Arvind Kejriwal condemned the Centre's police action against protesters during a march. Videos showed police using force against unarmed students, including young women. Kejriwal alleged the Modi government's actions were unprecedented and brutal. The Aam Aadmi Party launched a helpline for affected students and protesters. This follows clashes and detentions during the march towards Parliament.

title: Unmanned choppers, India's Varun and now Anduril's Thunder to change how we think about drones
content: Anduril Industries unveiled its autonomous attack rotorcraft Thunder at the Farnborough Airshow. This unmanned system is designed as a drone wingman for current and future attack helicopters. India also advanced its indigenous unmanned systems with the Varun Mk I Naval Shipborne UAS. Both developments highlight how unmanned rotary systems will become central to modern warfare. These systems offer flexibility and reduced risk to human crews in combat.

title: Soy Milk, Pasta And A Prison Plea: US Mercenary Wins Food Relief In Tihar Jail
content: Counsel for Matthew VanDyke argued at length before the court that his client had been unable to eat the prison diet because it was "spicy, oily, deep-fried and greasy", and that his medical condition required a different diet.

title: Rahul Gandhi To Sit Outside PM's House Overnight, Sends 'Join Protest' Message
content: Congress workers, led by Rahul Gandhi, Mallikarjun Kharge and Priyanka Gandhi, marched to PM Modi's residence to protest the paper leak issue on Tuesday.

title: 20-Year-Old Woman In ICU After Being Injured At CJP Protests In Delhi
content: The woman, who remains on ventilator support, collided with a barricade during police crackdown on protesters at Jantar Mantar.

title: CJP Sacks Spokesperson Over Burger Video. "I'm Not Accountable," He Says
content: The CJP said in a statement that it "strongly condemns the deeply insensitive actions" of Vijeta Dahiya, whose videos surfaced on social media during Monday's march.

title: Ex-Cop, His Family Beat Farmer To Death Over Land Dispute In Rajasthan
content: The accused, a neighbouring retired ASI, wanted to possess the land and was pressuring the victim, Sushil to transfer it in his name.

title: 70 km To 500 Metres: Two New Ladakh Bridges To Boost Border Tourism
content: A new motorable bridge in Ladakh's Nubra Valley is expected to reduce a nearly 70-km journey to a crossing of about 500 metres, making remote border villages easier to reach for residents and tourists.

title: Explained: Why Ladakh Is Facing Extreme Wet And Dry Spells
content: 2024 was the only year in this period when rainfall was not in the large-excess category with Ladakh receiving 17 per cent more rain than normal.

title: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: Congress Protests Outside PM's House, Centre Reaches Out
content: CJP Parliament March LIVE Updates: The Delhi High Court has allowed activist Sonam Wangchuk to be moved immediately from the state-run Safdarjung Hospital to Medanta in Gurugram.

title: 144 Refugees Dead Or Missing Since Mauritania Maritime Incidents: UN Report
content: The UN refugee agency UNHCR said that overall, three rescue and disembarkation operations were conducted between July 14 and 18 in Nouadhibou, Mauritania, bringing 387 people of various nationalities safely ashore.

title: Methane Gas Leak Triggered Sikkim Tunnel Collapse That Killed 10: Sources
content: At least ten people have been reported dead in the incident, while 12 have been rescued and over 18 are still feared trapped.

title: Char Dham Yatra Resumes In Uttarakhand After Temporary Suspension
content: According to the officials, the pilgrimage, suspended on Monday, has been smoothly restarted following a necessary review of the travel routes.

title: Pepper Spray, Knife Attack: Criminal Killed At Relative's Birthday Bash
content: Police suspect that Nandanavanam Suri's murder was an act of revenge linked to the killing of Mohammed Salman in Meerpet on June 7, 2024.

title: France Sees Worst Wildfire Season Since Record 2022 Blazes
content: The devastated area is about four times the size of Paris. And the area burned so far in 2026 has already far exceeded the total for all of 2025. France's all-time annual record stands at 66,300 hectares burned in 2022.

title: One Launch A Month Our Next Goal After Vikram-1 Success: Skyroot CEO
content: Pawan Kumar Chandana said SpaceX reached orbit only on its fourth launch attempt, while Skyroot succeeded on its very first attempt.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Kaveri To J-20, How China Overtook India In A 40-Year-Old Race To Build Fighter Jets
content: Both launched ambitious military jet engine programmes during the 1980s. Both depended heavily on Soviet and Russian technology. Four decades later, however, the finish line tells two dramatically different stories.

title: "Can't Ignore Concerns": Court Allows Shifting Sonam Wangchuk To Medanta
content: Earlier this week Wangchuk was forcibly removed from the CJP's protest site at Jantar Mantar in central Delhi, and admitted to Safdarjung, after the Delhi High Court directed authorities to 'do what needs to be done to save his life'.

title: 3-Lender Rule, Rs 2 Lakh Cap: How Microfinance Firms Curbed Bad Loans
content: After several quarters of decline, the sector's Gross Loan Portfolio (GLP) returned to growth in the quarter ended March 31, 2026.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Settles 238 Points Lower Amid US-Iran Tensions
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Crude oil prices retreat from 1-month high amid US-Iran war tensions; Brent at $88 per barrel.

title: AI Is Now Fighting AI And China May Have An Edge
content: A rogue AI agent launched a cyberattack on Hugging Face, a popular platform for open-source AI models. The company fought AI with AI.

title: Thousands regroup at CJP's Jantar Mantar protest site; Rahul leads Congress protest outside PM residence | LIVE
content: Govt wasted our time, says Dipke on talks between Nadda, CJP team; Delhi High Court allows Sonam Wangchuk to shift to Medanta Hospital

title: CJP's protest 'nautanki' to defame govt, create anger in youth: Maharashtra Minister Bawankule
content: Defending Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan senior BJP Minister Bawankule calls out CJP’s protest a drama to defame the government

title: Tamil Nadu government appoints V. Siva as Chairman of T.N. Housing Board
content: Mr. Siva is TVK’s North Chennai district secretary and was the party’s in-charge for the Perambur Assembly constituency from where Mr. Vijay was elected to the Assembly.

title: Statehood imperative for restoring J&K's special status: Chief Minister
content: ‘Some people tell us to talk about [restoration of] Article 370 and J-K's special status first... I want to ask them what does Article 370 or 371 mean without statehood,’ asked Jammu and Kashmir CM Omar Abdullah

title: Satish Jarkiholi urges youth to stay away from drug addiction
content: The Police Department, educational institutions, parents and the public should work together to eradicate the drug menace from the society, the Minister says

title: Assamese film Rador Pakhi wins special jury award at Moscow festival
content: The film is inspired by an Assam-based writer who pursues her dream despite suffering from spinal muscular atrophy

title: ‘Chalo Lok Bhavan’ protest in Hyderabad calls for Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan’s removal
content: Leaders of student organisations said the protest was part of a nationwide campaign highlighting concerns over the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, alleged examination irregularities, paper leaks, and the broader crisis in the education sector

title: Tamil rapper Arivu detained during bid to protest against NEET near Secretariat in Chennai, CM Vijay meets him
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay invited Arivu — who is best known for his Tamil independent music including the hit song Enjoy Enjaami, Thalapathy Kacheri from ‘Jana Nayagan’ — to his chamber and listened to the singer’s concerns.

title: Proposed property tax revision under CURE an “outright tax extortion”, says Telangana BJP chief Ramchander Rao
content: Mr. Rao questions collecting an application fee of ₹100 and an earnest money deposit (EMD) of ₹10,000 from each applicant of Indiramma Housing scheme despite the presence of lakhs of applicants

title: Char Dham Yatra resumes in Uttarakhand
content: Although the Char Dham Yatra was resumed, all departments have been instructed to keep a constant watch on weather conditions and travel routes so that immediate action can be taken if necessary

title: Calcutta High Court grants interim protection to TMC MP Mahua Moitra in hate speech case
content: TMC MP Mahua Moitra prayed before the court for an interim order preventing coercive police action in the case and quashing of proceedings against her in the case registered at Hogolberia police station in Nadia district

title: Allahabad HC delivers split verdict over bulldozer action on homes, properties of accused persons
content: Judge cites corruption, including donation theft at Ram Temple in Ayodhya, to reprimand State for taking over the top actions; the case arose from a petition filed by three residents of Uttar Pradesh’s Hamirpur district who alleged that their residential house and commercial establishments had been sealed and were under threat of demolition

title: Left MPs visit Jantar Mantar, demand Amit Shah's statement in Parliament over police action
content: There is a virtual martial law in this country; the brutality unleashed on innocent youngsters is unheard of; their agitation is for the country, but they were brutally attacked, said CPI(M) Rajya Sabha MP John Brittas

title: Justice Shree Chandrashekhar recuses from hearing bail plea of Bhima Koregaon accused Surendra Gadling
content: This marks the third recusal by a Supreme Court judge from hearing activist Mr. Gadling’s bail plea without assigning any reason; the bail plea arises from the 2016 Surjagarh arson case; Mr. Gadling has been in Taloja Central Prison since June 2018 following his arrest in the Elgar Parishad-Maoist links case

title: Why Tamil Nadu and Karnataka are sparring over Cauvery water? | Explained
content: As deficient rainfall revives the Cauvery water dispute, this explainer examines the water-sharing framework, the legal regime, reservoir status, and the unresolved issue of distress sharing

title: Rahul, Priyanka lead Congress leaders in protest outside PM's residence
content: Karnataka CM D.K. Shivakumar and Kerala CM V.D. Satheesan also attend protest outside PM residence in Delhi

title: Gujarat records 49.79 million-tonnes drop in carbon emissions in 2025-26
content: The reduction coincides with a record 67,655 million units of electricity generated from renewable energy sources

title: Qatar Airways ordered to pay ₹10 lakh to Kannur family for denying boarding to minors
content: Minors were denied boarding during a return journey from Kochi to Italy via Doha. The family had to later obtain fresh travel documents and transport both children back to Italy separately, incurring additional expense

title: Gachibowli and Khajaguda flyovers targeted for completion by May 2027
content: Foundation works, piers and pier caps have reached advanced stages at IIIT junction. The works are under progress at Khajaguda junction.

title: Khajaguda traffic snarls worsen as flyover and underpass works squeeze road space for 3 lakh vehicles daily; diversions soon
content: Raidurgam Law and Order Police Station to make way for the proposed underpass as road widening and utility-shifting gather pace

title: IIT Roorkee warns students against social media support to 'political movements like current one'
content: While the fresh advisory created furore on social media with several users terming it a "gag order", the institute insisted that it is in accordance with existing rules and should not be viewed out of context

title: Martyrs' Day rally: Mamata calls for INDIA unity against BJP in Bengal, rules out return of TMC rebels
content: Though she did not name the Congress or the CPI(M), her remarks were seen as an appeal to the TMC's INDIA bloc partners, with whom it has maintained an uneasy relationship in West Bengal despite being part of the national opposition alliance

title: Russia in touch with New Delhi over killing of Indian ship crew in Black Sea, Kremlin says
content: New Delhi summoned the Russian chargé d’affaires over the ‌strike on Golden Leo, a Guinea-Bissau-flagged ship manned by a ‌crew from India ​and Syria

title: Tamil Nadu scales up ABHA creation, 2.54 crore people covered so far
content: ABHA, a 14-digit unique health identifier created using an Aadhaar number, is a key feature of the Union government’s Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), launched in 2021, enabling a digital health records ecosystem

title: Farmers stopped at Punjab-Haryana border en route to Delhi rally against India-U.S. trade deal
content: Farmers wanted to peacefully go to Delhi and participate in the rally but the BJP-led Haryana government has stopped them by erecting barricades, Sarwan Singh Pandher, farmer leader and coordinator of the Desh Bachao Morcha, says

title: Cauvery Water Management Authority to meet on July 22 amid Tamil Nadu’s growing deficit in water realisation
content: Since June 1, the start of the current water year, four reservoirs of the Cauvery basin have received around 30 tmc ft, whereas Tamil Nadu has not even received four tmc ft, says an expert in Chennai

title: Supreme Court constituted Aravalli committee opens 21-day window for feedback
content: The development has come amid concerns about the committee’s neutrality, as it mainly comprises ex-government officers

title: Death of BDS student Nithin Raj: Kerala Home Minister seeks report on 'flawed arrest' of accused Dr. Ram
content: Ramesh Chennithala directs State Police Chief to conduct departmental inquiry into the reasons that resulted in Monday’s 'flawed arrest'

title: Six-month UDF rule in Kerala’s Pala municipality ends with ouster of chairperson Diya Binu
content: No-confidence motion, moved by LDF against Diya Binu, regarded as youngest chairperson of any civic body in the country, was carried with support of 17 councillors, including four Congress members and UDF vice-chairperson

title: Pothundy double murder case: a death penalty verdict with rare focus on rehabilitating victims’ family
content: Court directs District Legal Services Authority, Palakkad, to initiate rehabilitation measures for orphaned children of the victims; legal experts say such a specific direction was rare in criminal judgments

title: Centre reiterates release of Samagra Shiksha funds for Tamil Nadu tied to implementation of NEP
content: The Union government, however, says the RTE (Entitlements) component will be released in due time.  It was replying to DMK MP Dayanidhi Maran’s question in the Lok Sabha

title: Doctor, home guards among 19 held in Chhattisgarh for faking snakebite deaths, siphoning off lakhs
content: Each member of the racket played distinct roles to execute the fraud between November 2019 and February 2024, shows investigation

title: GBA engineer assaulted during footpath clearance drive near Shivajinagar, 10 persons arrested
content: Authorities said all those involved in the attack will be booked under Section 307 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS), which deals with attempt to murder

title: India-U.K. FTA creates predictability for businesses on both sides: City of London policy chief
content: The India-U.K. trade deal came into effect on July 15, 2026. In a written interview with The Hindu, Chris Hayward, Policy Chairman of the City of London Corporation, the U.K.’s financial and commercial hub, discusses the implications of the agreement, including what it means for manufacturing, infrastructure and financial services, as well as how investors can navigate changes in U.K. political leadership.

title: Railway police launch probe into clash between woman passenger and TTE in Kerala
content: Argument over upgradation of a general ticket to a reserved one. Following the incident, both filed complaints with the Government Railway Police, with a conflicting version of the incident

title: Balakrishna tears knee muscle in fall on ‘NBK 111’ sets; to undergo minor surgery
content: The actor slipped during an action sequence on the sets of ‘NBK 111’ at the Kakinada deep-sea port and tore a muscle in his knee; director Gopichand Malineni said he needs minor surgery and asked fans not to worry

title: Ten killed in methane gas blast at NHPC tunnel in Sikkim’s Namchi district; rescue operations under way
content: According to reports, a methane gas explosion triggered the tragedy at the tunnel at Samardung in the district’s Jholungey area

title: Resolve service issues of Waltair Division employees after split, DRUCC member asks Railway Board
content: Chalasani Gandhi said the split of the Waltair Division into the Visakhapatnam and Rayagada divisions had left about 18,000 employees facing confusion over seniority, promotions and transfers, and sought a joint procedure order

title: Government took ‘strict action’ against NEET paper leakers: PM Modi
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has said that the government took immediate action against those involved in the recent NEET question paper leak.

title: BJP leader Nainar Nagenthran condemns police officer for ‘threatening’ Thoothukudi journalists
content: A police officer made aggressive gestures and reportedly threatened journalists in Thoothukudi when they attempted to get the comments of arrested Vilathikulam DMK MLA G.V. Markandayan.

title: Parliament Monsoon Session Day 2 highlights: Both Houses adjourned for the day amid Opposition protests
content: LoP Rahul Gandhi along with some Opposition MPs met Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, urging him to allow a discussion in the House on the “brutality unleashed” on students

title: Woman, two daughters killed in house fire at Delhi's Lodhi Colony
content: At 10.21 a.m. Delhi Fire Services received a call informing about fire in a residential building in south Delhi's Lodhi Road

title: Supreme Court frees Kerala Waqf Board from State official ‘supervision’
content: A three-judge Bench headed by CJI Surya Kant passed the order while hearing the Board’s appeal against an interim order of the Kerala High Court on July 15

title: Gujarat flags off 139 new buses for tribal students under Birsa Munda transport scheme
content: The buses have been introduced jointly by the Tribal Development Department and the Gujarat State Road Transport Corporation (GSRTC) at a cost of ₹33.84 crore

title: Trinamool factions, Congress plan show of strength in Kolkata on Martyrs’ Day
content: Each group claims to be the ‘original’ flagbearer of Martyrs’ Day; all three meetings have been arranged within a 3 km radius; senior leaders have been trying to pull in crowds

title: Thunderstorms forecast in 11 Telangana districts on July 22
content: Strong surface winds are very likely to prevail at isolated pockets in the remaining districts of Telangana

title: Telangana launches Indiramma Housing scheme in Hyderabad; releases eligibility, application procedure, beneficiary contribution details
content: The flats with built up area of 528 sft will be built in multi-storey towers. While Telangana government would provide subsidy of ₹5 lakh, tentative contribution of beneficiary is ₹6 lakh. A flat will be spread over 400 sft.

title: No decision to increase ethanol blending with petrol beyond 20%: Petroleum MoS
content: Suresh Gopi, Minister of State for Petroleum also informed no decision had been taken to blend ethanol with diesel for commercial usage

title: Adult unmarried daughter can claim financial support for education from father under DV Act: Karnataka High Court
content: In a landmark ruling, the Karnataka High Court has affirmed that an adult unmarried daughter can seek financial support from her father for educational expenses under the Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005.

title: US student visa rules are changing on September 15: Who could be affected and how
content: Abhishek Mishra is a Digital Content Producer at The Times of India, working with the news desk. He writes with a keen eye on foreign policy, defence developments, and the shifting currents of geopolitics-and yes, Indian politics always finds a way onto his radar too. Read More
